message:
test(frankenterm-core): pin redactor coverage on ft robot send audit path (ft-0ctwe)

Adds an in-tree storage::tests block exercising
AuditActionRecord::redact_fields(&Redactor::new()) directly, so the
"every secret-bearing field on a send-path audit record gets scrubbed
before persistence" invariant is anchored on the redactor itself rather
than on the indirect record_audit_action_redacted_with_cx storage
helper. A future refactor that drops the redactor call from the
storage path will still fail this test.

New tests in crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs (added to the
existing #[cfg(test)] mod tests block following the autocommit-discard
test, ~125 lines):

- make_send_audit_record_with_secret helper builds a baseline
  send-path AuditActionRecord (actor_kind="robot", action_kind=
  "send_text", policy_decision="allow") with the given secret embedded
  in decision_reason / input_summary, mirroring the shape main.rs
  produces from `ft robot send <pane>`.
- Per-row assertions cover the secret-pattern matrix the production
  Redactor::new() recognizes — Anthropic / OpenAI / GitHub / Slack /
  generic bearer tokens etc. — verifying that each one is replaced by
  the standard "[REDACTED]" placeholder in every sensitive field.
- A negative-case assertion runs the same redactor against a record
  whose fields contain only safe text ("send authorized", "ft robot
  send 0 'ls -la'") and pins that the redactor leaves them byte-for-
  byte unchanged, guarding against an over-eager future redactor
  pattern.
- Comment block explicitly cross-references the sibling redactor
  coverage tests added in ft-3se13 (decision-log scrub) and ft-3xek9
  (newer-provider tokens), and instructs future contributors to add a
  row here whenever a new provider pattern lands in Redactor::new().

Test-only addition in the same file as the invariant under test — no
production logic, no schema changes. Marker comment header records
the bead id (ft-0ctwe) so the suite can be located when the bead is
revisited.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
